title: Isanthus brachiatus
intro: Isanthus brachiatus, commonly known as Isanthus or American Isanthus, is a perennial herb native to the eastern United States, belonging to the Gentianaceae family. Traditionally, Isanthus brachiatus has been used in herbal medicine to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Isanthus brachiatus include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Isanthus brachiatus is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other herbs in respiratory formulas and cough syrups.
